title: Advice from People Who Shouldn’t Give It : Effective Mentorship
description: For those in the role of advice-giving, there are moments and ways when advice can be very helpful, AND moments and ways when advice is NOT helpful. Based on his song by the same name, Ben Bailie Johnson shares memorable stories and his take on how to provide advice that’s sound, appreciated, useful, and delivered in a way in which it can be well-received.

title: Advocacy 101: How to Become a Better Advocate
description: Do you want to influence policy that you're passionate about, but you're unsure how to start? This hands-on and interactive workshop will teach you all the basics of advocacy, including: the different levels of advocacy, strategies to get others invested, how to obtain sponsorships, and how to grow the scale of your advocacy.

title: AI Fluency and the New Creative Economy
description: AI disruption is shifting the world from an economy focused on technical skills to one that values creative vision and strategic judgment above everything else. Learn from Ringling College's Creative Technologies faculty how to cultivate technological and AI fluency alongside innovative thinking, building the adaptability to take advantage of the entrepreneurial opportunities others miss.

title: Align: Stop Guessing Your Future – Smarter Career Decisions with AI
description: Choosing a career shouldn’t be a guessing game. In this workshop, students will explore Align, an AI-powered web app that moves beyond traditional career quizzes by analyzing real experiences and decision-making patterns. Through a live demo and hands-on activities, participants will upload their activity list, engage in interactive simulations, and work through real-world scenarios. Align then translates these inputs into skill profiles and personalized insights, helping students identify high-fit career paths and clear, actionable steps, from extracurriculars to skill building activities, to pursue them with confidence.

title: Amplify Student Success Using MBA Research Resources and AI Tools
description: Discover how to combine MBA Research resources with emerging AI tools to better prepare students for classroom learning and CTSO competitive events. Learn practical strategies for using study guides to align instruction with the expectations of competitive events, and explore how AI can support lesson planning, generate practice activities, and help students build the skills needed to succeed in both the classroom and competition.

title: Band Together
description: Step into a fast-paced networking challenge where participants are randomly paired and tasked with creating a personalized friendship bracelet that captures their partner’s personality. After crafting, each person takes the spotlight to present their desig n and explain why it perfectly represents their match. It is a fun, high-energy experience focused on creativity, connection, and networking beyond social media.
organization: Executive Council & National Secretary’s, Executive Council

title: Be the Federal Reserve: Control the American Economy
description: Have you ever wondered how one Federal Reserve decision can influence jobs, loans, businesses, and the economy? In this interactive workshops, participants will step into the role of the Fed and explore how interest rate changes affect inflation, employmen t, and growth.

title: Beach Blanket Bulls & Bears: The Summer Stock Market Game
description: Learn how your money can work for you this summer! Set your own goals and determine your own course to success and make your mark! SMG is a hands-on educational experience in investing where you’ll create and manage your own investment portfolio. You’ll pr actice investment research, learn the fundamentals, the rules, and immediately implement what you’ve learned. Join us at our session where we’ll play SMG’s companion activity – SMG InvestQuest live!

title: Before Your Hit the Floor: Master Your Elevator Pitch
description: Learn how to confidently introduce yourself, your ideas, and your organization in any professional setting. This session teaches the structure of an effective elevator speech —from the “intro before the intro” to a strong call to action. Participants will a nalyze examples, avoid common mistakes, and practice crafting a clear, engaging pitch they can use for networking, leadership events, and opportunities within Future Business Leaders of America and beyond.

title: Being a Student Leader
description: As student leaders, you are participating in organizations; leading meetings and events; assisting your peers; and building your resume along the way. This session will help to highlight how your experiences can be used to aid your career trajectory and wh at you choose to do after high school.
organization: George Mason, University-Costello, College of Business

title: Bringing Learning to Life with Real-World Project-Based Learning (PBL)
description: Project-Based Learning (PBL) is the instructional foundation of the High School of Business ™program. In this interactive session, participants will receive an overview of the program and experience how PBL creates engaging, real-world learning opportunities for students. Discover strategies for creating a student-centered classroom and leave wit h a ready-to-use project.

title: Build Your Personal Brand Before You Graduate
description: Your personal brand is what people say about you when you're not in the room. In this interactive session, students will learn how to intentionally build a personal brand that reflects their strengths, leadership style, and career goals. Participants will explore strategies for standing out in school, competitions, and future careers while building a professional reputation that opens doors for opportunities.

title: Build Your Personal Brand Like a Top Candidate
description: In a competitive environment where many students have similar grades, activities, and achievements, standing out comes down to how clearly you communicate your value. This interactive session will show you how to build a personal brand that gets you notice d, whether you’re applying to colleges, pursuing internships, or leadership opportunities. Students will learn how to identify their strengths, turn their experiences into compelling stories, and communicate their value with confidence. Through a live strengths quiz, hands-on activities, and peer collaboration, participants will leave with a clear personal brand statement they can immediately use in applications, interviews, LinkedIn profiles, and beyond.

title: Building Unshakeable Character for Modern Business Leadership
description: What separates a manager from a true leader? Drawing on 12 years in the U.S. Marine Corps and a PhD in Leadership Studies, learn that leadership is rooted in resilient character . This session explores key leadership pillars, including discipline, ethical decision-making, and mental toughness needed to succeed .

title: Built Different: Create Something that Matters – Build Your Capstone Legacy
description: Join us for an engaging and interactive workshop where you’ll learn how to turn your big ideas into actionable steps toward creating a meaningful impact. This session, hosted by a seasoned adviser with a capstone winner’s perspective, alongside a student w ho has been a finalist in the Capstone Competitive Review competition, will explore the journey of building something truly significant. In this session, you will: • Hear firsthand insights from an adviser and a capstone competitive review finalist about their experiences and the lessons learned along the way. • Participate in a Q&A session, where you can ask your burning questions about the capstone process, overcoming obstacles, and what it really takes to succeed. • Break down your own big idea into achievable steps using the “brick-by-brick” method: identifying the problem, taking action, and measuring impact. • Learn the importance of mentorship, teamwork, and persistence when building a lasting legacy.

title: Careers in the Creative Industries and Beyond
description: Are you interested in a career in the creative industries? Learn about the countless roles on the business side of creative organizations that combine creativity, strategy, and management – the design leaders, brand strategists, production managers, and entrepreneurs that make ideas happen. Gain insight into the typical development processes for creative projects and learn about resources for further information. Discover Ringling’s business degree designed for creatives and start exploring new opportunitie s within this field.

title: Changing the Conversation: From Fixing Problems to Fueling Possibility
description: This session introduces students to Appreciative Inquiry, a positive approach to problem-solving that focuses on strengths, possibilities, and what is already working well. Instead of only looking at what needs to be fixed, students will learn how to ask better questions, recognize their own strengths, and use those strengths to create goals for the future. Through discussion and activities, students will explore how a positive mindset can help them build confidence, work better with others, and take action toward the future they want.

title: COLLAB
description: Every Great Leader Has a Great Team, But This Requires “Networking”! Come And Join Dr. B As We Make Meaningful Connection and Explore Tools That Will Assist You In Finding Amazing People To Collab With To Make Your Destiny Possible!

title: College and Career Pathways in Business
description: Students will engage with faculty and representatives from AACSB member institutions to better understand the many pathways into business careers. The session will cover choosing majors, building skills in high school, and what makes a strong college appli cant. Through open discussion, students can ask questions and gain insight into how to turn their interests into meaningful career opportunities.

title: Community Impact Challenge: Preparing Students to Build AI Solutions for Real
description: In this interactive workshop, students and advisors will step into a real Community Impact Challenge experience. Using real examples from local businesses (tax, dental, bakery, academy), participants will choose a business, identify a problem, and design a n AI solution concept —no coding required.

title: Businesses
description: Through a guided activity, teams will map a simple customer flow, define what the AI agent would do, and see how students can build and deliver real solutions in just a few hours. Walk away with a ready-to-use plan to launch the challenge in your chapter —helping students gain real project experience, build portfolios, and create measurable community impact.

title: Confidence on Command: How to Speak Up, Stand Out, and Be Taken Seriously
description: If your heart starts racing or you avoid eye contact the second someone says “present this in front of people,” this session is for you. This session will teach you the core elements that make a public speaker effective from someone who has delivered over 350 presentations to audiences across the country. Together, we will explore the role confidence plays in public speaking, understand the primary purposes of speaking, and learn three practical strategies you can immediately apply in FBLA and everyday communication. This session challenges the idea that some people are naturally good speakers and instead shows that there are no bad speakers, only better ones.

title: Day in the Life Of – Marketing Research Analyst (Work From Home)
description: Participants will understand what the responsibilities and day-to-day work of a marketing research analyst who works from home look like. The presenter has over 10 years of experience working as an analyst, account manager, and sales director in the field. His experience includes working in-office and now full-time at home.

title: Designing a Brand: Core Visual Principles at the Dawn of AI
description: Products, services, and destination experiences begin relationships with audiences long before any purchase occurs. A consistent visual language is crucial to building engagement and trust. Learn the skills and conceptual approaches design teams use to cre ate branded materials that anticipate, meet, or exceed audience expectations, and your business goals, regardless of the technological tools involved.

title: Designing Virtual Reality for Business Application
description: Explore how virtua l reality is transforming business training, marketing, and collaboration in this hands-on workshop. Learn to create immersive experiences using free, no-code tools – no expensive equipment needed. Participants will discover key VR concepts, explore simple creation methods, see how AI can assist design, a nd leave with ready-to-use resources to start building right away.

title: Dream Big, Start Small: Turning Small Actions into Big
description: In this session students will understand how big goals are accomplished through small consistent action. Students will look at what obstacles they personally face in their everyday life that might hinder them from successfully accomplishing their goals.

title: Impact
description: This session will also talk about practical ways to form goals and the path to accomplish their goal that is manageable.

title: Dream It, Plan It, Get It: Career Positioning + Interview Skills
description: This seminar empowers high school students to take ownership of their future by aligning interests, strengths, and values with career goals. Students learn practical strategies for career positioning, skill-building, and experiential learning. Objectives i nclude developing self-awareness, creating a competitive profile, and mastering interview skills to confidently secure opportunities.

title: Dress for Success
description: Success starts before the interview. This is an engaging workshop that helps students understand how professional dress builds confidence, shapes first impressions, and supports their personal brand across academic, leadership, and early career experiences . Presented by Men’s Wearhouse, Dress for Success offers practical guidance on professional attire, including how to dress for different occasions, the importance of fit and presentation, and how style choices communicate readiness and respect. More than fashion advice, this session empowers students with real-world tips they can apply immediately – regardless of budget or prior experience. Participants will leave with a clearer understanding of workplace expectations, how to balance professional ism with personal style, and how to present themselves with confidence for internships, interviews, networking events, and beyond.

title: Effective Communication in the Workplace
description: Strong communication is an easy way to signal competence and professionalism. This interactive workshop looks at communication in many forms: emails, IMs, in-person, video conferences, etc., as well as how to handle difficult conversations that could come up in professional settings. You'll look at real examples, fix common mistakes, and leave with practical ideas you can use immediately.

title: Elevating CTE through the National Technical Honor Society
description: Elevate Career & Technical Education in your school while providing value-added opportunities to students through the National Technical Honor Society (NTHS). Learn to leverage an NTHS alongside your FBLA chapter to empower your students to pursue meaningf ul careers, access career resources ($335,000 in scholarships and more), and celebrate excellence in CTE. Visit us on the expo floor as well!

title: Entrepreneur Mindset: Build a Business in 20 Minutes
description: In this session, participants dive into a fast-paced entrepreneurship challenge, where they have to use innovation and critical thinking to develop a unique business plan in 20 minutes. At the beginning, the objectives of the session will be explained and they will be taught about the marketing side of business that they will explore. They will form teams and are given an industry with a real-world problem to solve, and they have to design a product or service for the scenario, identify key marketing princi ples for their idea, and develop a brand pitch to advertise and sell their company. They will use collaboration, innovation, and insightful thinking in order to successfully create a 30 second-1 minute business pitch with their team, in 20 minutes. Partici pants will then present this idea to the audience, and us speakers will give positive comments and further guidance. At the end, all participants will be given the opportunity to vote for the "most investable idea" out of the presentations, and the winner will receive a small reward.

title: Experience the AP Business with Personal Finance Pitch Competition
description: One of the most exciting parts of the AP Business with Personal Finance Course is through a course Business Canvas Project. In this project, students apply the content and skills they learn throughout the course into a business idea of their own culminatin g in a "Pitch Competition". Brin your students to this session to experience a modified version of this experience and also learn from FBLA advisers, who are piloting the course and developing resources, how this project aligns seamlessly with FBLA's compe titive events. Winner of the competition will receive a surprise "prize"!
organization: Schacter, Jo Powell, Jennifer Sheets, Jacob Avila, College Board

title: Explore.Act.Tell: A Business Case for FBLA Advisers to Empower Student Learners to Make a Difference
description: In this session, FBLA advisers and student leaders will learn about the essential project-based learning skills offered in the Explore.Act.Tell. program. Building off of FBLA's leadership, career readiness, project planning, and team collaboration, adviser s will receive curricular support to help students create a hunger project from ideation to implementation, measure results, and submit for possible grant and service recognition awards.

title: FBLA at the Bell: Free Bellringer and Competition Prep Resource
description: FBLA has collaborated with BusinessU & Stukent CTE to deliver a new bi-weekly bellringer resource to the Future Business Leaders of America! This session aims to provide a general overview of how FBLA advisers use this fun and FREE bell-ringer resource to kick-start classroom conversations, prepare for competitions, and inspire the next generation of business leaders!

title: FBLA MasterChef: Your Recipe to Success
description: Think you have what it takes to be the next MasterChef? Step into the kitchen of business communication! In this fast-paced workshop, you’ll learn the recipe for a winning speech, add the right ingredients for clear messages, and plate your pitch with confi dence. Through fun challenges and a team communication cook-off, prepare your dish, impress the judges, and take home the title of FBLA MasterChef Champion!

title: FBLA Olympics FICO’s Score a Better Future
description: Get ready for the FBLA Olympics, a fast-paced, team-based workshop where participants rotate through short, Olympic-style activities that build key leadership skills. Events include: • Ice Skating – Break the Ice: Practice strong introductions using clarity, confidence, and connection • Swimming – Swimming through Obstacles: Learn consistency and reliability through teamwork challenges • Breakdancing – Taking Action: Show initiative and creativity in hands-on tasks • Skiing – Skiing with Style: Build confidence and presentation skills Join FICO for an interactive workshop designed to help college students take control of their financial futures. Learn how credit scores work, what factors influence them, and practical steps you can take now to build a strong credit foundation. Whether you’re opening your first credit card, managing student loans, or just starting to think about your financial goals, this session will give you the knowledge and confidence to make smarter financial decisions as you move into the next chapter of your life.
presenter: Emma Lin, Param Thakkar, & Harleen Virk

title: Finding Your Fit
description: Through this session, attendees will hear from university recruiting individuals how to explore colleges and universities. The path to college is full of big decisions, but through this session the presenters will help to make them a little easier to proce ss. Come prepared with any general questions you have about searching for colleges to help make the presentation more engaging.
organization: George Mason University –, Costell College of Business

title: Fix Your Finances, from the Ground Up
description: Coming from an immigrant family that struggles with credit card debt and a lack of financial literacy education, I created a company with the goal of preventing this from happening to any other families that deal with that same problem. The goal of the wor kshop presentation is to talk about financial habits or goals that can help both immigrant parents that may attend, or students who want help learning how to plan for the future.

title: From Case Briefs to Closing Arguments: Careers in the Law
description: Explore the diverse pathways within the legal profession —from litigation and corporate counsel to public interest and judicial clerkships. Learn what lawyers actually do, the skills that matter most, and how to start building your legal career now.

title: From Classroom to Careers: Inspiring the Next Generation of Business Leaders
description: Today’s students are tomorrow’s leaders, and they need more than textbooks to get there. In this session, we’ll explore how iCEV’s dynamic business, marketing, finance, IT, and media curriculum helps educators spark curiosity, connect learning to real-world careers, and empower students with skills that matter. Walk away with fresh strategies to engage learners, align with industry expectations, and open doors to meaningful career opportunities.

title: From Dream Job to Day One: Reverse-Engineer Your Path to College and Career Success
description: Every great career starts with a plan, but most students don't know where to begin. In this hands-on workshop, you'll flip the script and start with the end in mind: your dream job. From there, you'll work backwards to map out what education or training yo u'll need, get a realistic look at what college actually costs, and build a scholarship strategy to help close the gap. You'll leave with a one-page personalized roadmap —your blueprint from where you are now to where you want to go. And with Encourage, the free college and career planning app, you'll have the tools to keep building that plan long after this session ends.

title: From FBLA to Agency:
description: Follow an FBLA alum’s journey from student leader to agency owner.

title: Skills to Own Client Work
description: Learn which FBLA skills translate directly to owning a services business, what skills to build next, and how to start a teen-friendly services business (freelancing, micro-agency, or local service) with low cost and high learning. Leave with a simple offer, outreach plan, and next steps to Make Your Mark.

title: From Hi to Hired
description: In today’s competitive world, your experiences matter —but knowing how to communicate them matters even more. In this interactive session, participants will work in teams to identify what makes the “perfect candidate,” reflect on their own leadership and ca reer experiences, and learn how to turn those moments into powerful interview responses. Attendees will leave with practical strategies to confidently market their skills, stand out to employers, and communicate their value in interviews and professional s et

title: From Me to We
description: Great leaders don’t focus on themselves —they focus on serving others. In this interactive session, students will explore the principles of servant leadership and how putting the team first builds stronger organizations and communities. Participants will le arn practical strategies to lead with empathy, empower peers, and create positive impact in their FBLA chapters, schools, and future careers.

title: From Middle to High School: Empowering Students through Career Exploration
description: Empower students to explore their futures with purpose-built resources for every stage of learning. This session highlights the importance of sparking career awareness in middle school and expanding that exploration into high school through standards-aligned courseware and industry-recognized certification pathways. Learn how immersive simulations and virtual internships bring business concepts to life, connecting classroom instruction to real-world application and helping students build confidence, career skills, and momentum for what comes next.

title: From Stress to Strength: Essential Tools Every Future Leader Needs to Thrive
description: From Stress to Strength: Equip future leaders with practical resilience and mental health tools to handle pressure, prevent burnout, and lead with clarity. Interactive peer discussion plus Q&A. Leave with a simple toolkit you can use now in school and late r at work to protect performance, strengthen culture, and build trust-saving time and money.

title: Fund Your College. Shape Your Future. Lead with Army ROTC!
description: Army ROTC workshop for FBLA students highlighting how the program develops leadership, discipline, and strategic thinking while earning a college degree. Learn about scholarships, career pathways, and how ROTC prepares future business leaders for roles in management, logistics, finance, and operations.

title: Fundamentals of Wage and Hour Compliance
description: The Wage and Hour Division is committed to helping employers and workers develop a greater understanding of federal labor laws. All workers deserve to feel secure that they are receiving their full, legal pay. All employers deserve to grow their business w ithout worry about unknown government regulations disrupting their goals. The Wage and Hour Division wants to prove that a helpful government should be the expectation and not the exception.
organization: United States Department, of Labor-Wage and Hour Division

title: Future Skills You Need in the Age of AI
description: Artificial intelligence is transforming how we work, lead, and innovate. In this session, students will explore the essential future-ready skills that remain valuable in an AI-driven world —such as critical thinking, creativity, adaptability, and leadership . Participants will learn how to combine human strengths with emerging technology to stay competitive in future careers and become leaders who can thrive alongside AI.

title: Future-Ready in 15 Minutes: Low-Prep College & Career Activities for FBLA Advisers
description: You have a lot on your plate. Between competitions, meetings, and everything else, carving out time for deep college and career programming can feel impossible. But what if you could meaningfully move the needle in 15 minutes or less? In this hands-on sess ion, advisers will experience a sampling of ready-to-use activities—including bell ringers, career grab bags, and work values exercises drawn from Encourage's free lesson plan library —that can be dropped into any chapter meeting with zero prep. You'll leav e energized, equipped, and with resources you can use the very next day.

title: Helping Students Focus – A Nobel Winning Concept
description: In today’s fast-paced classroom environment, helping students stay focused is more critical than ever. Based on Daniel Kahneman’s groundbreaking work in Thinking, Fast and Slow, this workshop offers personal finance, accounting, and business teachers’ practical strategies to improve student focus and decision-making. By understanding the cognitive processes behind how students think, you can more effectively guide them toward better learning outcomes.

title: Houston, We Have a Problem: Mission Control Escape Room
description: Step into Mission Control Houston to save a space-tech startup’s satellite network! In this interactive 45-min escape room, teams take on roles in technology, data analysis, finance, and communications fields to solve business problems, allocate resources, and stabilize systems by practicing teamwork, innovation, cybersecurity, and business communication.
organization: Ramaraju, & Sannidhi Palle, Centennial High, School

title: How Does AP Business with Personal Finance Align with FBLA?
description: The AP Business with Personal Finance course officially debuts globally during the 2026-27 school year. A number of FBLA advisers across the country are bringing this exciting course to their schools. Join us for this experiential session to learn what is driving the excitement and how the course aligns seamlessly with FBLA's competitive events programming. Discover the resources that will be available to help FBLA chapter advisers connect the course to specific components of these competitive events.
organization: Schacter, Jo Powell, Jennifer Sheets, Jacob Avila, College Board

title: How to Run a School Business
description: This workshop will introduce students to the fundamentals of operating a school store. Participants will explore the skills involved in planning, organizing, directing, and controlling business operations while gaining an understanding of the responsibilit ies and risks associated with managing a business. The workshop will feature hands-on activities and student-led discussions to encourage engagement and practical application of concepts.

title: How to Use AI Simulations for FBLA Competitive Events Prep
description: Danny Rubin, company founder, will show a new platform--available for free for all FBLA chapters--in which students practice Competitive Events with a virtual judge! Simulated Competitive Events prep includes; • Job Interview • Public Speaking • Business Communication • Entrepreneurship and more This is a can't-miss session that will give your chapter an edge and give YOU the latest tech tools.

title: Inspire
description: What do the most successful leaders in the world have in common? They inspire and empower. Learning about inspiration and empowerment is not an academic standard. You won’t find it on any test, but it's vital if you want to make an epic impact in the world . This high energy engaging experience will teach you simple tools you may apply to your FBLA chapter to increase engagement and recruitment. As you break out into your career, you’ll leverage this leadership quality to advance in your role or launch your own business. All you need to bring is a cell phone to engage with a few audience questions and a willingness to be a little silly as you dance and practice confidence building stances and communication skills with your peers.

title: Interactive Team Building
description: Join our state officer team for some fun and interactive ideas for team building.

title: Last Seven Standing: A High-Stakes Communication Simulation
description: In this fast-paced, high-pressure simulation, participants must persuade their peers to secure one of seven seats on a mission to save humanity. With shrinking time limits and no second chances, students practice persuasive communication, critical thinking , and decision-making. This interactive experience challenges participants to clearly articulate value, adapt messaging under pressure, and collaborate toward consensus in a dynamic, competitive environment.

title: Lead Loud: Middle School Leadership Basics
description: Leadership starts now--not in high school. This high-energy, interactive workshop introduces middle school members to the fundamentals of teamwork, communication, confidence, and goal setting. Through engaging group activities and simple leadership chall enges, students will practice speaking clearly, listening actively, and supporting one another. Participants will leave excited about FBLA, empowered to contribute, and confident in their ability to lead within their school and community.
presenter: James Fletcher, Lesira Kpea, Joe Lewis

title: Lead the Change: Thriving in Constant Disruption
description: Change is constant, from school clubs to future careers, and strong leaders know how to guide their teams through uncertainty. This session introduces practical change management strategies that help emerging leaders understand what changes are coming, lea d through it, and build foundational competencies for to be leaders of the future. Through examples and simple leadership tools, participants will build confidence to guide others, support their team’s adaptability, and support a culture that thrives in ev olving environments.

title: Lead, Strategize, Success: Army Careers for Future Business Leaders
description: An informative U.S. Army workshop for FBLA students highlighting career pathways in leadership, logistics, finance, technology, and operations. Learn how Army training develops skills in strategic planning, management, and decision-making, along with education benefits and long-term career opportunities.

title: Leadership Initiatives’ Live International Business Consultation
description: Leadership Initiatives will host a live international business consultation for FBLA students with real business leaders in the developing world. Engage in a dynamic experience as you collaborate on authentic advertising strategies for enterprises in sub-Saharan Africa. Gain insights into the realm of International Business and forge connections with accomplished business owners. This is your chance to explore the remarkable achievements of Leadership Initiatives Interns in advertising, finance, and busines s development, inspiring you to embark on your own journey in the world of international business and development.

title: Leading Early: How to Build Influence as a New
description: A workshop about stepping into leadership early, navigating responsibilities, and building influence even as a younger student.

title: Let Them Lead: Empowering Officers to Run the Chapter
description: Many FBLA advisers find themselves managing every detail of their chapter, leaving little room for student leadership development. This session focuses on practical strategies for transitioning from an adviser-run chapter to a student-led organization. Par ticipants will learn systems for officer structure, delegation, meeting management, and leadership accountability that empower students to plan events, run meetings, and drive chapter success while the adviser serves as a mentor and guide.

title: Let’s Talk About It… Passion & Purpose
description: There are BIG questions in life…. “What do I want to be when I grow up?” or “What’s my life purpose?” If you don’t have the answers yet, that’s okay! Join Dr. B in this inspiring session as we navigate your Passion and Purpose. You will explore with other attendees using practical tools and reflective strategies to connect your passions with a meaningful future, so you can move forward with clarity and confidence.

title: LinkedIn for Leaders: Build a Safe Digital Brand
description: Your online presence is often the first thing colleges, scholarships, and employers see. In this session, FBLA members will learn how to create a strong LinkedIn profile that highlights their leadership, FBLA achievements, skills, and experiences. We will also talk about internet safety and smart ways to manage a public profile. Students will learn what should and should not be shared online, how to protect their privacy, and how to build a positive digital footprint. The goal is to help students feel confi dent using LinkedIn and other platforms to tell their story and open doors to future opportunities.

title: Make Your Mark as a Leader in your Community
description: In this workshop students will take a look at what local communities really need and how they function, what leadership in their community looks like, and discuss legacy and what kind of mark they want to make on the communities they are involved in. Stude nts will also get a snapshot of what it looks like to work in an environment with varying ages, strengths, and personalities and how to build community despite these challenges. This session is great for students who hope to become involved in their commun ity now and as adults.

title: Make Your Mark: Where FBLA Leadership Begins
description: How will you make your mark in FBLA? This interactive workshop helps members turn involvement into meaningful leadership. Participants will explore how the theme Make Your Mark connects to the future of FBLA —Leaders Begin Here —by learning how to maximize o pportunities through competitive events, chapter leadership, service initiatives, and networking. Walk away with practical strategies to grow your leadership skills, strengthen your chapter, and begin your leadership journey in FBLA.

title: Mastering FBLA’s Official Event Study Guides
description: Explore FBLA’s multi-year initiative to modernize study guides for objective test events. Discover how these resources align with National Business Administration Standards, MBA Research materials, and third-party tools to support student success. Get a preview of upcoming efforts to extend this framework to FBLA presentation events.

title: Maximizing Impact through FBLA National
description: Discover how FBLA National Programs empower members to grow as leaders, strengthen career readiness, and elevate chapter success.

title: Programs
description: This interactive session explores key initiatives including the Business Achievement Awards (BAA), Champion Chapter, Competitiv e Events, Lead4Change, and more. Participants will engage in hands-on activities and leave with practical strategies to increase member involvement, chapter recognition, and measurable impact at the local and national levels.

title: Mission Impossible: Escape with NPEC
description: Join the National President’s Executive Council for a dynamic, thrilling, and once in a lifetime experience where you will unlock the tools to leverage teamwork skills as a high impact leader. Get ready to engage in an exciting mission impossible themed es cape room while gaining leadership skills such as communication, strategy, and problem solving. Accept your mission and lead with confidence.

title: Mission Matcha – Create, Mix, and Brew Your Leadership Skills
description: You have been assigned a mission: A mission to make matcha! You must make James and Kavin’s matcha orders, and you better do it quickly… Work with your team through each of the 3 steps of matcha making and leadership activities to see who can be the most efficient at making the best matcha. If you focus, collaborate, and work diligently, your hard work will be rewarded!
organization: Mountain Plains Region, Council, & North Central Region Council

title: Networking with the Stars
description: Step into the ballroom and test your networking abilities through a series of different competitions and activities. Members will learn about the importance of networking and how to improve their networking skills. But make sure to give it your all in ever y performance and activity because our panel of judges will be watching and scoring you throughout the workshop. Remember that your effort could be rewarded with the legendary Mirrorball Trophy!

title: Networking within Your Community with Teens Teach Tech
description: Providing a unique take on networking, the Teens Teach Tech team will walk attendees through utilizing community resources to build a business network that will support them as they progress in their chosen industry. Accompanied by active TTT FBLA chapter members, this workshop will also provide insight into the fun and innovative way TTT connects chapter members across the country with a common goal-the service of others in a digital world.

title: No More Drama
description: Where There Are People There Will Be Challenges! Challenges are Healthy Obstacles That Require A Strategic Approach For Guaranteed Success. Join Dr. B In a Very Serious Conversation About Finding The Source Of Conflict And How To Resolve It In a Way That Saves You And Your Personal And Professional Relationships!

title: No-Nonsense Networking
description: The No-Nonsense Networking workshop, presented by the National FBLA Southern Region Council (SRC), is designed to help members build meaningful professional connections with confidence and purpose. Participants will learn practical strategies for starting conversations, making lasting impressions, and leveraging networking opportunities at conferences, in their communities, and in their future careers. Through engaging discussion and real-world tips, this session breaks down networking into simple, effectiv e steps that any FBLA member can use to grow their professional circle and open doors to new opportunities.

title: Ok, but What about STEM? How FBLA Advances Your STEM Career Goals
description: Students pursuing STEM careers often overlook the value of business skills. This session explores how public speaking, networking, and interviewing skills developed through FBLA help students communicate research, stand out in competitive healthcare admiss ions, and gain a major advantage in fields like medicine and science.

title: Own the Room: Public Speaking Skills for Future Leaders
description: This session will cover practical tips for public speaking in everything from formal speeches in front of a crowd to making the most of the dreaded everyday small talk. Students will learn about how to structure a formal or informal speech, how to deliver it, and how to present yourself and your speech to the audience. Students will get practical tips on managing the nerves that come with public speaking. Students will leave this workshop better equipped to handle everything from a formal speech, to an inte rview, to small talk at a dinner party. Great for students who feel intimidated by public speaking and also students who want to polish their skills. Skills learned in this workshop can be used in both a professional and everyday setting.

title: Pathways to a Powerful Tax Career: Becoming an Enrolled Agent
description: Have you ever wondered who helps people and businesses solve complex tax problems or represent them before the Internal Revenue Service? In this engaging session; you’ll discover the exciting career path of becoming an Enrolled Agent (EA). An Enrolled Agent is a tax expert who is officially authorized by the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) to represent taxpayers across the United States. EAs help individuals, families, and businesses prepare taxes, resolve tax issues, and navigate complicated financial situations. They are trusted professionals who understand tax laws and help people make smart financial decisions.

title: Personalities in Life and Business – Soft Skills for the 21 st Century Educator and Business Leader
description: The keys to motivating learners and business colleagues partially lie in how we interact with different personality types. Teachers tend to teach to their personality type, often a pitfall that can be counterproductive in education. This session will exami ne four unique but common personality types and how educators and business practitioners can optimally engage with others with different personality types. Caution: This session might change your life!

title: Pressure Makes Presence
description: This workshop teaches performing impromptu. After a brief lesson on public speaking and projecting confidence, participants compete in a high energy activity. Randomly selected speakers receive a word and must speak for as long as possible while staying en gaging and apt. The catch: no filler words (“um”, “like”, etc). If used, their timer stops. The winner is judged on duration and speech quality.

title: Say It Like You Mean It: Speaking to Lead
description: Unlock the secrets to captivating public speaking in "Say It Like You Mean It: Speaking to Lead." Conquer your nerves and deliver powerful speeches with confidence. Dive into strategies that boost your presence and forge genuine connections with your audie nce. Perfect for competitions, presentations, or pitches, this workshop arms you with the skills to speak clearly and convincingly. Get ready to lead —and leave a lasting impression —through your voice!

title: Seat at the Table: The Art of the Professional Presence; How to Own the
description: How do you own the room when you're the youngest in it? "SEAT AT THE TABLE" is a masterclass in Executive Presence. This session teaches emerging leaders how to project confidence, credibility, and executive presence in competitive and professional environ ments.

title: Room When You’re the Youngest in It
description: Through language shifts, delivery techniques, and interactive demonstrations, attendees will learn how tone, posture, and word choice influence perception —helping them communicate ideas with authority in interviews, presentations, networking, and le adership roles. Respect isn't about your birth certificate; it’s about your signals.

title: Secrets to Scholarship Success
description: Being a high achiever is more than academic success. In this session, students will learn how to approach scholarships strategically by telling their story and identifying opportunities that set them apart. We’ll also share insider tips on what reviewers l ook for and creative ways to search for scholarships. Participants will leave with tools to strengthen applications and prepare for a successful college transition.

title: Self Advocacy for School and Life
description: This session would cover the role of self advocacy for students inside and outside of the classroom. This session would focus on real world examples of my life going from a high school student, to getting through college and eventually becoming a teacher a nd FBLA co-adviser while overcoming the challenges of Autism Spectrum Disorder and Anxiety Disorder. Session would give students techniques and skills to use either as they continue in secondary school or are moving to the job force or post-secondary caree rs.

title: Sell Me This Pen
description: Sell Me This Pen is a once-in-a-lifetime opportunity for students to compete and learn sales techniques, getting real-time feedback on what they’re doing right, and how they can do it better. It’s a competition to see who can sell random items the best, al l inspired by a true Wall Street sales story, and combined with plenty of fun.
organization: Kannap pan, Jonah, Bayog, Shourya, Bangaru, Claire Ding, Nayla Sharma, &, Akulnand Sreelesh

title: Share Your Story: Alzheimer’s Association Fundraising Sharing with a Purpose (SWAP)
description: Are you ready to make a difference and enhance your leadership skills? Join us for an exciting workshop where you will learn how to harness the power of storytelling to boost your fundraising efforts and support the Alzheimer's Association Ready to level up your conference experience? Sharing with a Purpose (SWAP) is back, but bigger, faster, and more impactful than ever. Hosted by the National Secretary's Executive Council (NSEC), and presented by three groups of students, this three-year t radition brings together some of the most passionate members from across the country, all selected through a competitive application process. Here's the twist: instead of sitting through just one session, you'll experience three high-energy, student-led workshops, all within one single time block. That means more ideas, more perspectives, and more takeaways in less time. But more importantly, it creates space for ideas to be actively shared and SWAPPED, not just heard.
organization: Alzheimer’s, Association, National Secretary’s, Executive Council

title: Shark Tank FBLA: The Ultimate Pitch Showdown
description: FBLA Shark Tank is an exciting, interactive workshop where students team up to create innovative new products and business ideas. Teams will step into the “Tank” and pitch their inventions to the Sharks, who will evaluate each presentation and choose the u ltimate winner. Does your team have what it takes to impress the Sharks and take home the grand prize?

title: Simulated Internships &
description: Stukent CTE & BusinessU offer 13+ cutting-edge “Simternships,”

title: Curriculum for the Classroom
description: which provide students with hands-on, work-based learning experience and application. Dive into various simternships including: Social Media Marketing, Intro to Business, Entrepreneurship, Personal Finance, Accounting, Digital Marketing, Sports Marketing, HR, Supply Chain, (new!) Generative AI and review aligning curriculum & certifications .

title: Speed Dating – The Art of the Elevator Pitch and Mastering Confidence Spend Less, Travel More!
description: In this activity, members will have short “speed dating” activities where they are paired randomly with other attendees where they learn impromptu speaking and how to carry themselves with confidence while improving their public speaking skills. Through various prompts, attendees will present to one another about this and be able to speak confidently when in a situation of impromptu speaking Ready to see the world on a student budget? Join Peter Frantz for a highly interactive workshop! Discover actionable hacks to slash airfare using apps like Hopper and Skiplagged; master hotel price matching at Hilton and other hotel brands worldwide; and unlock massive savings on ultra low-cost carriers. Packed with live polls, you’ll walk away with the ultimate budget travel toolkit!
organization: Dave Jacob, & Nithya Talluri, Ohio FBLA, Peter Frantz, Federal High, Administration

title: Stop Doing More: How to Stand Out without Doing Everything
description: High school students are often told to do more to stand out, join more, add more, and stay busy. But more is not always better. Better is better. In this interactive workshop, you'll will learn what actually matters when it comes to the job search. Led by a former Fortune 100 recruiter who has reviewed over 50,000 résumés and hired 500 candidates, this session breaks down how to position your experie nces in a way that stands out. You will also learn how to ask better questions, think more critically about your future, and leave with a practical framework you can use to explore what comes next with confidence.

title: Stop Scrolling, Start Selling: Organic Brand Growth
description: Learn how to turn social media scrolling into real sales —without spending money on ads. In this interactive session, you’ll see how a student-run jewelry brand built a loyal community and generated profit in under a year. Through hands-on activities, you’l l create content ideas, practice engagement strategies, and leave with a clear plan to start growing your own brand today.

title: Stukent CTE: Business, Generative AI, and Hospitality Resources
description: Learn about Stukent CTE’s (BusinessU + Stukent) expanded, world-class resources: courseware, 13+ Simternships (hands-on simulations), and Industry-Backed Certifications. New content includes Hospitality & Tourism and Generative AI. Certifications are approved in 15+ states and align with 3rd-party certs. Also featuring FBLA at the Bell, a free bell-ringer and competition-prep resource.

title: Teaching Accounting: Debits, Credits, & Balance Sheets, Oh My!
description: Join us for an interactive workshop showcasing BusinessU & Stukent CTE’s high school accounting course, simulations, & (new!) certifications. Stukent CTE explores standard accounting concepts, including t-accounts, journalizing/posting, worksheets, and mor e, as well as advanced concepts. Embedded throughout is the language of business, and a finance “bent”.

title: The 3D Blueprint: Discover. Design. Develop.
description: The most successful teams don't wait for the new year to figure things out... they map it out in advance. In this interactive session, participants will step into the role of strategic leaders to craft a vision for the year ahead using the 3D framework: Di scover, Design, Develop. First, define a clear vision for chapter success and learn how year-end planning drives membership growth and chapter engagement. Then, participants will design strategies for recruitment, national program participation, fundraisin g, and community service through collaborative brainstorming. Finally, develop an effective and measurable Program of Work that positions the chapter for a strong start to the upcoming membership year.

title: The Art of Influence: Mastering the Power of Persuasion
description: Influence is the key to success in almost every area of life. It’s your secret weapon for mastering persuasion, building powerful relationships, and getting what you want in business and life. Backed by psychology and real-world tactics, Dr. Candace Esken will explain how to win people over authentically, communicate with impact, and create lasing influence. Whether you’re closing deals, leading teams, or simply looking to level up your social skills, this game-changing workshop will give you the edge. Stop hoping for opportunities and start creating them where none existed before. In a world driven by relationship and perception, influence isn’t just important – it’s essential.

title: The Art of Risk Taking; Calculated Moves in a Competitive Market
description: This session explores the strategic decision-making behind the world’s most pivotal business moves: from intentional losses to billion-dollar risks that redefined industries. Attendees will examine real-world case studies before applying their knowledge in a live stock market simulation, where every decision directly impacts their portfolio in real time. Participants will leave with a deeper understanding of risk, strategy, and what it truly takes to lead boldly in business.
organization: Rentala, Vihaan Shan, & Noah Ahuja, Obra D. Tompkins High, School FBLA

title: The Business of Being You
description: In this interactive personal branding workshop, participants will explore professional branding frameworks used across industries and learn how to apply them to personal branding, audience strategy, and influence-building. Students will be guided through reflection, journaling, discussion, and applied strategy exercises to define their unique voice and presence.
organization: Savannah College of, Art and Design

title: The Furious Five Formula: Building Powerful Teams in FBLA
description: Just as the Furious Five work together to protect the Valley of Peace, FBLA members rely on each teammate’s strengths to succeed. Like Po, who learns to embrace his abilities and trust others, we achieve more by valuing what everyone brings. With open communication and guidance – just as Master Shifu teaches – members can overcome changes, build trust, and create a supportive environment where all can succeed. Join the FBLA National Officers to learn the secrets to building powerful teams!

title: The Leadership Rodeo
description: Saddle up for The Leadership Rodeo, a high-energy Texas-themed workshop hosted by the Eastern Region Council (ERC) and Western Region Council (WRC). In this interactive session, participants will rotate through “rodeo events,” each designed to strengthen k ey leadership skills. At the Bull Rider Decision Round, teams tackle leadership scenarios under pressure. The Team Lasso Challenge focuses on teamwork through fast-paced problem-solving tasks. In Pitch the Ranch, participants develop and pitch a Texas-inspired business idea, while Quick Draw Communication tests clear communication as partners recreate designs using only verbal instructions. Teams will earn “rodeo points” to win a prize.
presenter: Pari Malla, Lesira Kpea, Eastern Region

title: The Mindset of High-Performing Leaders: Building Confidence, Discipline, and Decision-Making Skills
description: Success in business leadership is not just about knowledge it’s about mindset, discipline, and the ability to make strong decisions in complex situations. In this engaging session, Behavioral Strategist Darrick Lamar shares powerful insights on the habits and thinking patterns of high performing leaders. Students will explore how self-awareness, emotional intelligence, and resilience contribute to effective leadership and long term success. Participants will gain actionable strategies for strengthening focu s, improving decision-making, and developing the confidence needed to lead teams, manage challenges, and pursue ambitious goals in business and life.

title: The paper Quest: Decoding Business Files
description: Think you have what it takes to find the solution? You team has been handed a high-priority portfolio, but here's the catch: the files are encrypted and scattered! The clock is ticking. To secure this deal, you must complete a series of tasks with a group of friends that will test your knowledge of business fundamentals. Solve the case, and you'll secure a ticket to the next boardroom meeting!

title: The Power of a Dollar: Building Long-Term Wealth
description: Join this workshop to master long-term wealth! Discover why starting early is key and how $10,000 can become millions. Learn stock market basics, Roth IRA benefits, and actionable tips to build skills, start earning, and avoid debt. Start now!

title: The Wellness Edge: Rethinking Workforce Success
description: As workforce landscapes evolve, organizations recognize success requires more than technical skills —it requires nurturing the whole person. This session explores integrating wellness with workforce development to improve retention, productivity, and leader ship growth while equipping participants with practical tools to build healthier, high-performing workplace environments.

title: Think Twice: Why Smart People Make Bad Choices
description: This session introduces students to Daniel Kahneman’s Thinking, Fast and Slow and explores how people make decisions in everyday life. Students will learn the difference between fast, automatic thinking and slower, more thoughtful decision-making. Through real-world examples, they will examine how shortcuts, assumptions, and biases can influence choices at school, work, relationships, and leadership. The session will help students recognize common decision-making traps and develop strategies to think more c learly, make better choices, and avoid being misled by first impressions or emotions.

title: Thunderbird Information Session and Interactive Entrepreneurship Activity
description: Join Erin Workman, Director of Global Recruitment at Thunderbird School of Global Management at ASU to learn more about the Bachelor of Global Management and Bachelor of Science in International Trade. This session will include an interactive activity expo sing students to entrepreneurship concepts.
organization: Thunderbird School of, Global Management |, ASU

title: Top 3 Mistakes High School Students Make on their Résumakes High School Students Make on their Résumé (and How to
description: You've planned events, led your peers, and maybe even competed BUT does your resume highlight the impact of your experience in FBLA (and beyond)? Too often, students undersell themselves with vague task-based bullets that don't articulate their true value. In this session, you'll learn from a former Fortune 100 company recruiter who has reviewed 50,000+ resumes and hired 500+ students.

title: Fix Them)
description: Discover the top three mistakes students make and how to fix them, so you can showcase your involvement as an advantage in your job search!

title: Turning Ideas into Impact, The Entrepreneurial Mindset in Business School
description: AACSB member school participants will share real examples of startups and innovative projects, while students brainstorm their own ideas and receive feedback. The session emphasizes creativity, problem solving, and how to take the first steps toward bringi ng ideas to life.

title: Uniting for a Cause: FBLA & Alzheimer’s Association
description: Join us for an exciting and interactive workshop where we’ll dive into the powerful partnership between FBLA and the Alzheimer’s Association! Together, we aim to spark positive change by harnessing our unique strengths and passions. We will discuss how Alzheimer’s impacts communities and how YOU can make a difference. Additionally, we will highlight everyday actions that can improve brain health and present the latest research, along with more ways to get involved with t he Alzheimer's Association. Don’t miss this opportunity to make an impact – your involvement truly matters!

title: W!se in the Classroom – Supporting Personal Finance Instruction
description: Join me to learn how to unlock the full potential of the W!se Financial Literacy Certification Program. We’ll explore how to effectively integrate the program into your curriculum, utilize our comprehensive educator tools, and ensure your students earn a credential that proves their financial readiness for the real world.

title: We Came here to Have Fun (Essentials for Effective Adventure )
description: No one wakes up thinking, "I want to have a boring life." Most people long for a life of adventure, fulfillment, and joy, but the idea of getting there often trips us up. After 20+ years as a professional wanderer, Ben shares a talk about emotional health, the importance of community, and 8 secrets for turning any journey into a worthwhile adventure. Bonus: This talk will feature live music!

title: What Employers Really Want: 5 Essential Skills Every High School Student Needs (and How to Teach Them)
description: High school students gain real leadership experience in FBLA-but many struggle to communicate it in the job search. This session equips advisers with the tools to help students translate their involvement into skills that align with employer expectations . Using NACE's 2024 Career Readiness Competencies, attendees will learn how to coach students beyond buzzwords and into clear, confident storytelling. You'll leave with a resume template, interview prompts, and programming ideas to add to existing events!

title: What is Your Leadership Style
description: The presentation will examine many speaker styles that can influence how we can lead from the front or the back of an organization . There will be practical examples of various international leaders and their how their styles influence countries and organizations .

title: Where Profit Meets Purpose
description: Learn how you can support others in the reuse economy while fundraising and making a huge environmental impact.

title: Youth@Work – Workplace Rights
description: The Interactive Youth@Work session from the U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC) introduces future leaders to their rights and responsibilities in the workplace and explores how they can help build fair, inclusive, and harassment-free organi zations from day one. Through real-life scenarios, brief video clips, and discussion, participants will examine common discrimination issues young workers face, practice responding as both employees and emerging leaders, and learn concrete steps to prevent and address problems before they escalate.
